product_images.description: Front view of Vulcan OmniPro 220 showing LCD display, control knobs (Left, Main, Right), Home/Back buttons, power switch, positive and negative sockets at bottom, and MIG gun socket
Inside panel view showing wire spool area, wire feed mechanism, feed tensioner, idler arm, cold wire feed switch, and the settings/reference chart on the inside of the door

elements: MIG Gun / Spool Gun Cable Socket
front_panel_controls.description: Labeled diagram of front panel showing all controls and socket positions

interior_controls.description: Labeled diagram of interior showing wire feed system components

polarity_dcen_flux.description: DCEN setup for Flux-Cored: Ground Clamp in Positive Socket, Wire Feed Power Cable in Negative Socket

polarity_dcep_mig.description: DCEP setup for MIG: Ground Clamp in Negative Socket, Wire Feed Power Cable in Positive Socket

polarity_tig.description: TIG setup: Ground Clamp in Positive Socket, TIG Torch in Negative Socket, Foot Pedal connected inside, Gas hose to regulator

polarity_stick.description: Stick setup: Ground Clamp in Negative Socket, Electrode Holder in Positive Socket

feed_roller_types.description: Feed roller showing V-Groove (solid core) with 0.025 and 0.030/0.035 grooves, and Knurled Groove (flux-cored) with 0.030/0.035 and 0.045 grooves

wire_spool_loading_small.description: 1-2 lb wire spool loading: Wingnut, Spacer, Spool Brake Pad, Spool Spindle

wire_spool_loading_large.description: 10-12 lb wire spool loading: includes Spool Adapter between spindle and spool

gun_cable_connector.description: Correct vs incorrect Gun Cable Connector insertion into Wire Feed mechanism. Incorrect = not fully seated, will leak gas.

elements: Push angle (0-15° for MIG solid wire)
welding_angles.description: MIG gun angles for different weld types and wire types

stringer_vs_weave.description: Stringer bead (straight line, narrow weld) vs Weave bead (back and forth, wider weld)

tig_torch_assembly.description: Exploded view of TIG torch assembly showing all components

electrode_grinding.description: Tungsten electrode grinding technique: angle against grinding wheel, conical tip 2.5x electrode diameter, grinding direction parallel to length

duty_cycle_diagram_mig_120v.description: 120VAC: 40% at 100A = 4 min welding, 6 min resting. 100% continuous at 75A.

duty_cycle_diagram_mig_240v.description: 240VAC: 25% at 200A = 2.5 min welding, 7.5 min resting. 100% continuous at 115A.

duty_cycle_diagram_tig_stick.description: TIG 120V: 40% at 125A. TIG 240V: 30% at 175A. Stick 120V: 40% at 80A. Stick 240V: 25% at 175A.

weld_diagnosis_wire.description: Visual comparison of wire weld beads: Good Weld, Voltage Too Low, Voltage Too High, Travel Speed Too Fast, Travel Speed Too Slow, CTWD Too Long/Wrong Polarity. Each with photo and correction.

weld_diagnosis_stick.description: Visual comparison of stick weld beads: Good Weld, Current Too Low, Current Too High, Weld Speed Too Fast, Weld Speed Too Slow, Arc Length Too Short, Arc Length Too Long. Each with photo and correction.

weld_penetration_profiles.description: Cross-section profile views showing inadequate penetration, proper penetration, and excess penetration/burn-through for both wire and stick welding

wiring_schematic.description: Full internal wiring schematic showing AC input, bridge rectifier, PFC, IGBT, transformer, MCU board, LCD screen, wire feeder, solenoid valve, fans, and all connector pinouts (CN1-CN8)
wiring_schematic.note: Complex electrical diagram — best surfaced as the original manual page image rather than regenerated

settings_chart.description: How to Choose a Welder chart: 6-step decision matrix comparing Flux-Cored, MIG, Stick, and TIG across skill level, shielding gas needs, material type, thickness, applications, and weld cleanliness

quick_start_guide.page_1: Front side: 6-step quick start for wire welding (spool loading, wire feeding, tension test, settings)
quick_start_guide.page_2: Back side: Cable setup diagrams for Stick, MIG, Flux, and TIG — labeled numbered diagrams showing exactly which cable goes where

interactive_tools_to_generate.description: User selects process (MIG/Flux/TIG/Stick), voltage (120V/240V), and amperage. Tool shows duty cycle percentage, welding time, and rest time in a visual 10-minute clock diagram.
User selects process. Tool shows an SVG diagram of the front panel with colored cables going into the correct sockets, plus gas requirements.
User inputs: process + material type + material thickness + wire/electrode size. Tool outputs recommended wire feed speed, voltage, gas type, gas flow, and polarity setup.
interactive_tools_to_generate.data_source: Synthesized from specs.json + procedures.json + selection chart

interactive_tools_to_generate.description: User describes symptom. Tool generates an interactive decision tree walking through possible causes and solutions.
User describes weld appearance or selects from visual examples. Tool identifies the problem and shows the correction with before/after comparison.
Interactive version of the selection chart. User answers questions about skill level, indoor/outdoor, material, thickness, and cleanliness needs. Tool recommends the best welding process.
